item.title: uwu big brother...
item.content:encoded: Really loving the idea that the Sunrise Collective is the big brother of the SCP Foundation who let the SCP Foundation feel like they won over a big bad villain so they would leave the Sunrise Collective alone. Not sure what exactly the Sunrise Collective does besides that, considering all the terrible apocalypses (and ZK-Class Scenarios that affect all realities) that happen without their intervention.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This has to be one of my favorite skips in awhile. The idea of there being a larger greater Foundation makes sense considering the nature of the SPC Universe, and I rather enjoy the 05's Council reaction to a rival. It seems that precisely because the Sunrise Collective share the Foundation's goal, the Foundation treats them more harshly than other GOI. Because in the end they are rivals, competing to do the exact same thing. I am not sure how I feel about the last bit with the Foundation 'victory' over the Collective. I imagine that we should either feel remorse if we destroyed a largely benevolent organization that saw us interlopers, or fear if the Sunrise Collective is still functioning and we essentially just poked the bear. I don't really feel either however.

Overall one of my favorite skips!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This doesn't work for me because the Sunrise Collective is basically a Much Cooler Than You version of the Foundation, using a lot of tropes we've seen constantly (for the better part of a decade) with SCP fan characters who easily outsmart the Foundation and are much more powerful than the Foundation could imagine.

That takes a really excellent execution to pull off, but it's just kind of … here. It's not bad, it's just here.

It only helps a little that the Foundation "wins"(?). I wasn't rooting for either side. Honestly, every voice in the article came off a little samey.

I'm holding off on voting just in case I find time to read the related material and find this a compelling ending with context, but fair warning, reading the comments thread indicates a bit of the opposite…

Recommending a new take on this concept after the contest is over.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
(I don't see how the idea that humans are colonists could coincide with SCP-1000 without some serious revision) .

Nah, it's perfectly compatible. Though I don't outright state it in the article, SCP-1000 performed multiple uncontrolled rewrites of reality (to stave off apocalypses, presumably), before the SK-class dominance shift. Who knows what the original timeline looked like? Who knows if there was an original timeline? Is that even a meaningful question based on an accurate perception of reality?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wasn't a fan of this one for a few reasons. First up, I don't see the point of the colour format at all, or for the computer jargon at the front and end of the article. One of these seems to be there to justify the other, but I can't tell which and I could do without either.

There are way too many crosslinks here that I haven't read, so I feel like I'm missing half the information I need, especially this thing they are getting the information from. There are implications resulting from some of these crosslinks (humans being colonists on Earth) that by themselves would probably be enough for me to downvote in some situations, but this also seems to contradict some of the other crosslinks (I don't see how the idea that humans are colonists could coincide with SCP-1000 without some serious revision) .

Ultimately though, the biggest issue is I just got nothing from the concept. There's some bigger, more powerful group out there doing the same work as the Foundation. They say they can destroy the Foundation, the Foundation for some reason thinks blowing up a single island will solve that issue and (surprise surprise) it doesn't. There seems to be a lot of telling rather than showing here; what evidence is presented that this group is actually as powerful as they say, besides a single information campaign that could have been pulled off by any other group with knowledge of the Foundation?

This reads like an attempt to set up a group for ongoing work more than anything else, and I'm having a hard time seeing how this even fits the theme of history at all. -1 from me.

Here are some minor issues I spotted:

Issues
– hide block

At this time, we must act as if SCP-4000 is aware of global Foundation activity.

Why, if it's listed as Neutralised?

Investigations into the locations associated with sightings of SCP-4000 would continue, without success, over the next year.

This line reads like prose rather than part of a Foundation document.

rose sharply before dipping out.

"dipping out" is very nonclinical.

SCP-4000 suspected behind event.

This sentence reads like it's missing a few words.

The point was to make as little of waves as possible.

"as few waves as possible"

Remember why they we are doing this,

Extra word in here.

item.title: I truly enjoyed this skip
item.content:encoded: A really well done skip IMHO. It has my +1. However, I suggest you to add any "Proof of Might" around the Collective, to give any reason why they are so massively strong.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is definitely one of my favourites in the contest. Unique, a full story that kept me interested throughout, and the right amount of mystery. Only issue is I don't see why the old-style computer format needed to be used, especially throughout. Still an easy +1 though.

item.title: That was really good.
item.content:encoded: I have only liked a few of the 4000 proposals that have came in so far. This one is one of them. Its just amazing. Take my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Absolutely brilliant. Upvoted happily!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The green on black is a pain to read, and then when you get to the white boxes it just looks wrong.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That, and monospace fonts aren't good for very much other than code imo.

I still enjoyed it a lot, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know that they lost. Them winning in this version is what led me to make my original comment. I feel like they're too overpowered, although Mary Sue is the wrong term.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I hate to say it, I like what you did with the format for verisimilitude's sake, but man, that contrast is killer on my eyes and I just don't want to read it. D:

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! If I think of anything I can add to spruce it up, I will. I kinda suspected portraying them in such a negative light, as interlopers, might not go down perfectly. But I like it, and as long as others like it, it was worth it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: See, this is the point though, they are overreacting, it's supposed to feel slightly out of character, because they are being defensive, they know the collective knows their secret, pretty much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This isn't substantially different from the last one to me, even with the new addendum at the end. The Collective still feels to me like we are being told that they're a bigger badder Foundation; the panic that leads the O5 to do what they do does not feel earned and there are no stakes to the conflict.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Yes. There's no other way about it. We cannot co-exist, because you must be contained."

"You are an extraterrestrial threat to this world, its ecosystem."

I thoroughly enjoyed this aspect of the scip. Sure, the Sunrise Collective might not be the most interesting GOI, but the concept of humanity being an anomaly in the greater scope of things nails the "History" aspect of this contest perfectly.

Even though a lot of people seem split on this as it is, this is my personal pick for the contest for now, although that might change with later entries.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The thing is, they originally lost, but I listened to the people that said that needed to be changed and now it's the opposite. I'll consider it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I second this. It's hard for me to care about the conflict between the Foundation and the Collective when it's almost certain that the Collective are going to win from the moment the reader learns about them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I had the same problem. Basically, the Collective has no substance other than just "bigger badder Foundation". All the other GoIs do something differently. Broken god is trying to rebuild Mekhane, AWCY makes anomalous art pieces, the GOC curb-stomps things instead of containing them. The Collective is different from the Foundation pretty much literally only in that they are bigger, older and stronger. In addition, they act all high-and-mighty, which while being the logical actions for them, really grates on a lot of readers. I do like the concept, but I think you just need to add some flavoring to it. Make the Sunrise Collective stand out, be its' own unique take.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A Mary Sue is an idealised character… these guys aren't a Mary Sue. I would know, as I wrote the Mary Sue to end all Mary Sues

Would you care to go into more detail with what made you think they are Mary sues?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You took my biggest problem with this, that the collective is the GOI equivalent of a Mary Sue, and made it worse.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Take two, thanks to everyone in chat and in the forums, Toa_Vine especially, tatob910 for the neutralized logo.

There's still a good bit of implication and subtext, but I managed to almost double the length.
